subject: Re: 1.09 crashes when starting "Process only this items CFT"
body:
Hi Dmitri,
it was indeed a settings issue: When checking "Use Movie Sum" for the CTF 
estimation it worked. Obviously, the Thon rings of the single frames did 
not have sufficient quality. Those of the whole image were ok. Now it is 
running. Many thanks,

Ute

teg...@gmail.com schrieb am Freitag, 6. November 2020 um 12:53:48 UTC+1:

> Hi Ute,
>
> my next guess would be that the CTF doesn't have enough 0-crossings to fit 
> (that is what usually causes the exception you shared). Do you see Thon 
> rings if you do an FFT (e.g. in Digital Micrograph if you have a Gatan 
> camera)? Does the micrograph have particularly low defocus? What kind of 
> data is it?
>
> Cheers,
> Dimitry
>
> On Friday, November 6, 2020 at 8:36:48 AM UTC+1 ute.hei...@googlemail.com 
> wrote:
>
>> Hi Dmitri
>>
>>  
>>
>> thanks for your quick response. I have checked before that the decimal 
>> sepator is a dot. But now I changed from German to English (Great Britain) 
>> and all digits are visible and separated with a dot. When using English 
>> (Germany) also all digits can be seen but separated with a comma. Manually 
>> changing the separator to dot results in no decimal places behind the 
>> separator. So far so good.
>>
>>  
>>
>> Unfortunately, the crash remains also with correct decimal settings.
>>
>>  
>>
>> Best regards,
>>
>>  
>>
>> Ute
>>
>> Hi Dmitri
>>
>>  
>>
>> thanks for your quick response. I have checked before that the decimal 
>> sepator is a dot. But now I changed from German to English (Great Britain) 
>> and all digits are visible and separated with a dot. When using English 
>> (Germany) also all digits can be seen but separated with a comma. Manually 
>> changing the separator to dot results in no decimal places behind the 
>> separator. So far so good.
>>
>>  
>>
>> Unfortunately, the crash remains also with correct decimal settings.
>>
>>  
>>
>> Best regards,
>>
>>  
>>
>> Ute
>>
>>
>> teg...@gmail.com schrieb am Donnerstag, 5. November 2020 um 22:44:40 
>> UTC+1:
>>
>>> Hi Ute,
>>>
>>> could you please change the system's regional format from German to 
>>> English to make . the decimal separator? I've never seen it crash 
>>> completely on German systems, but let's try to fix the easier problem first 
>>> in case it solves the issue.
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>> Dimitry
>>>
>>> On Thursday, November 5, 2020 at 10:31:53 PM UTC+1 
>>> ute.hei...@googlemail.com wrote:
>>>
>>>> I just installed Warp 1.09 on our PC (Nvidia Quadro P4000 with 8GB RAM, 
>>>> Windows 10 Enterprise, CUDA 11, GPU driver 441.66 -> 452.39 cannot be 
>>>> installed due to older windows version 1709)
>>>>
>>>> The initial values (i.e. pixel X/Y) can only be set without decimal 
>>>> place which seems strange to me. When changing to "Fourier Space" and 
>>>> starting "process only this items CTF" the software crashes:
>>>>
>>>> System.IndexOutOfRangeException: Der Index war außerhalb des 
>>>> Arraybereichs.
>>>>    bei Warp.Tools.Helper.Subset[T](T[] values, Int32 fromInclusive, 
>>>> Int32 toExclusive) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Tools\Helper.cs:Zeile 718.
>>>>    bei Warp.Movie.<>c__DisplayClass294_3.<ProcessCTF>b__9(Int32 i, 
>>>> Int32 threadID) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Movie.cs:Zeile 1564.
>>>>    bei Warp.Tools.Helper.<>c__DisplayClass48_0.<ForCPU>b__0(Object id) 
>>>> in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Tools\Helper.cs:Zeile 678.
>>>>    bei System.Threading.ExecutionContext.RunInternal(ExecutionContext 
>>>> executionContext, ContextCallback callback, Object state, Boolean 
>>>> preserveSyncCtx)
>>>>    bei System.Threading.ExecutionContext.Run(ExecutionContext 
>>>> executionContext, ContextCallback callback, Object state, Boolean 
>>>> preserveSyncCtx)
>>>>    bei System.Threading.ExecutionContext.Run(ExecutionContext 
>>>> executionContext, ContextCallback callback, Object state)
>>>>    bei System.Threading.ThreadHelper.ThreadStart(Object obj)
>>>>    bei Warp.Tools.Helper.Subset[T](T[] values, Int32 fromInclusive, 
>>>> Int32 toExclusive) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Tools\Helper.cs:Zeile 718.
>>>>    bei Warp.Movie.<>c__DisplayClass294_3.<ProcessCTF>b__9(Int32 i, 
>>>> Int32 threadID) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Movie.cs:Zeile 1564.
>>>>    bei Warp.Tools.Helper.<>c__DisplayClass48_0.<ForCPU>b__0(Object id) 
>>>> in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\Tools\Helper.cs:Zeile 678.
>>>>    bei System.Threading.ExecutionContext.RunInternal(ExecutionContext 
>>>> executionContext, ContextCallback callback, Object state, Boolean 
>>>> preserveSyncCtx)
>>>>    bei System.Threading.ExecutionContext.Run(ExecutionContext 
>>>> executionContext, ContextCallback callback, Object state, Boolean 
>>>> preserveSyncCtx)
>>>>    bei System.Threading.ExecutionContext.Run(ExecutionContext 
>>>> executionContext, ContextCallback callback, Object state)
>>>>    bei System.Threading.ThreadHelper.ThreadStart(Object obj)
>>>>
>>>> What is the issue?
>>>> Thank you very much for your response.
>>>>
>>>> Best regards,
>>>> Ute
>>>>
>>>
